danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct character sprites from Looney Tunes canon (Elmer Fudd, Marvin the Martian, Yosemite Sam, Tasmanian Devil, Wile E. Coyote)
danger_signals.meaning: Boss encounters and level threats; iconic villain appearances signal narrative progression and escalating danger
Range-based threat; requires evasion or counter-attack strategy

danger_signals.visual_form: Robot enemies with electrical visual effects
danger_signals.meaning: Close-range contact is lethal; maintain distance communication

danger_signals.visual_form: Black cats crossing Bugs' path, followed by falling safes or horses
danger_signals.meaning: Cartoon logic hazards; interaction with black cat triggers cascading environmental damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Duck Dodgers character with visible laser blaster
danger_signals.meaning: Specific enemy variant is unkillable; signals must-avoid state rather than engage state
Scarce resource that becomes rarer in later levels; visual indicator of vulnerability increase

safety_signals.meaning: Restores health; presence indicates safe moment to reassess strategy

safety_signals.visual_form: Special power-up items (stun or instant-kill variety)
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invulnerability or threat elimination; signals moment of tactical advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Rightward or downward progression toward level exit
safety_signals.meaning: Movement direction (left-to-right or vertical descent) telegraphs safe path progression

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Looney Tunes protagonist sprite with distinctive ears and carrot-based health
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player-controlled character; contrasts visually with all other entities through animation style and scale

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Animator character; appears in final level with multiple visual alter-egos (Drip-Along Daffy, Robin Hood Daffy, Duck Dodgers)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Three distinct versions based on paint-spill world state; Duck Dodgers variant is visually marked as invincible
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Controls the painting/game world structure; transformation variants signal narrative escalation

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Named iconic characters (Elmer Fudd, Marvin the Martian, Yosemite Sam, Tasmanian Devil, Wile E. Coyote, Toro the Bull)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss-tier versions of traditional antagonists; cartoonish visual style maintains franchise consistency
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appear as level-specific threats; franchise-based recognition signals level theme and difficulty scaling

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Black cats, pigs with slingshots, robots, safes, horses
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mixed generic and thematic hazards; robot enemies distinguished by electrical visual effects
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Distributed throughout levels; black cat triggers cascading hazards; pig and robot placement telegraphs hazard zones

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Daffy Duck paints Bugs into a game world
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bugs trapped in painting; levels structured as visual scenes from Looney Tunes shorts
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Bugs must traverse level-based environments to progress toward final confrontation with Daffy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Daffy manifests in two distinct visual forms based on paint remaining: Drip-Along Daffy or Robin Hood Daffy
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss form changes determine attack patterns and defeat conditions; Duck Dodgers appears as unkillable stage hazard

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Carrot collectibles become visually sparse in later levels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player resource management becomes critical; visual rarity signals difficulty curve and heightened threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Levels alternate between left-to-right traversal and top-to-bottom descent structures
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Different environmental navigation requirements; visual layout communicates required strategy shift

environmental_storytelling.example: Levels based on original animated shorts (e.g., 'Bully For Bugs' featuring Toro the Bull); visual and enemy design references source material to establish narrative context
Black cat superstition hazard (safes, horses) translates cartoon logic into game mechanic; visual presentation maintains tonal consistency with source franchise
Named villain characters appear as level-capstone bosses; visual distinctiveness of each villain (Elmer Fudd, Marvin, etc.) signals narrative progression through Bugs' rogues gallery
Daffy Duck's three alter-ego forms (standard, Drip-Along, Robin Hood) represent narrative branching based on player performance; visual variant communicates outcome state

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Level threat; boss-tier combat encounter based on 'Rabbit Rampage' short source material
Projectile attack pattern via slingshot; ranged threat requiring evasion or counter-strategy
Close-range electrocution hazard; visual electrical effects signal lethal contact zone
Crossing path triggers superstition-based cascade (falling safes, horses); non-combatant but cascade-initiator
Cannot be defeated; one-hit kill laser attack; visual behavior signals avoidance-only interaction
Drip-Along Daffy or Robin Hood Daffy appears based on paint-spill progression; each variant has distinct attack pattern
Requires out-maneuvering; behavioral pattern learning required for damage
Requires strategy-based defeat; described as requiring out-thinking and out-maneuvering

notes: This game uses Looney Tunes IP to create a narrative wrapper around a platformer structure. Visual communication relies heavily on character recognition (established villain sprites) and level theming tied to animated short sources. World state is communicated primarily through boss variant appearance (Daffy's multiple forms) and resource scarcity (carrot health). Danger signals are mostly character-based (enemy type recognition) rather than environmental color-coding. The game lacks faction complexity—essentially one protagonist versus multiple named antagonists. Final level structure (paint spill mechanic determining Daffy variant) represents the primary dynamic world-state-to-visual mapping. Environmental storytelling is license-driven: cartoon logic hazards (black cat superstition) maintain franchise tone while doubling as mechanical challenges.
